channel.title: AI on Jamie Craane personal blog
channel.description: Recent content in AI on Jamie Craane personal blog

item.title: Parsing, Visualizing and Analyzing Log Data with Claude Code
item.description:
Introduction

A production system feels sluggish, you suspect the database, and you have a log file full of slow query entries staring back at you. The data you need is right there. The hard part is turning a few thousand lines of text into answers.

Recently I had exactly this situation: a log file from production containing slow database queries, and four questions:

  • Which queries are slow?
  • What is the distribution of the execution time?
  • When are the queries slow?
  • What are the most frequently executed queries?

These are the bread-and-butter questions of database performance work. And yet, answering them well is surprisingly awkward with the tools we usually reach for.

item.title: Bridging Human Intent and AI Code Generation with @AIGuideline
item.description:
Introduction

As AI-powered coding assistants become increasingly prevalent in software development, a new challenge emerges: how do we ensure that AI-generated code aligns with our project’s specific architectural patterns, design principles, and coding standards?

Existing AI guidelines, for example junie/.guidelines exist for this purpose. However, they are often large and challenging to maintain. Depending on the project, they can also contain a lot of information. The larger the guidelines file, the harder for the AI to follow consistently.
